question.content: For the given simply supported beam, determine the selected I-beam model from the strength check. Answer format: a single final model designation; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Set the model variables and sign convention first: upward reactions \(F_{R,A},F_{R,B}\), downward loads \(q\) and two concentrated loads \(F\). The loading is symmetric with respect to the span center.
step_1.key_point: Symmetry allows equal-reaction simplification before detailed section checks.

step_3.key_point: Moment equilibrium gives the right support reaction.

step_4.key_point: Force equilibrium gives the left support reaction.

step_5.content: Draw the shear diagram from left to right using reaction jumps and the UDL slope. Then integrate shear to obtain the bending-moment diagram.

step_6.content: From the bending diagram, take \(M_{max}=45\,\text{kN*m}\). Compute required section modulus: \(W_x=\frac{M_{max}}{[\sigma]}=\frac{45\times10^3}{160\times10^6}=281\,\text{cm}^3\).

step_7.content: Choose 22a as a preliminary section because \(W_x=309\,\text{cm}^3>281\,\text{cm}^3\).

step_10.content: Final selection: 25b. It satisfies both bending and shear checks.
